transcript: I'm Kwon Park, I'm the Global Head of Digital Asset Partnerships at Crypto.com. So, you know, Crypto.com publicly set forth a very ambitious roadmap this year. And, you know, thankfully and fortunately, we were able to deliver some of these products earlier than we expected. Um, you know, some highlights include the growth of our stock and ETF trading, the launch of credit cards, sports and event contracts, and custody in the United States, the Level Up program, and a revamp of our main app to improve our user experience. And this year, we continued to expand our support across the Solana ecosystem. Specifically, we added many Solana-based projects to our main app and exchange, allowing our users to have access and to help raise awareness for the Solana ecosystem. And we're committed to continuing that trend into 2026. You know, Crypto.com has also powered Solana in various ways across our entire product suite. We continue to raise awareness through marketing campaigns through our main app, exchange, and our Earn programs in eligible jurisdictions. You know, Crypto.com supports SPL native support for USDC, USDT, and PYUSD stablecoins on both the main app and exchange. You know, Solana can be used to pre-fund our flagship debit card and to pay credit card balances where cards are available. Custody and OTC execution services are available in eligible jurisdictions for Solana and its ecosystem projects. And regulated products such as Solana Strike and UpDown Options are available in the US under our Designated Contract Market license, a DCM with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission. You know, looking ahead, you know, Crypto.com Derivatives North America, which is a CFTC-licensed exchange, you know, we were able to pioneer the compliant crypto derivatives and event trading products. And the company became the first major crypto platform to secure a Futures Commission Merchant, a DCM, and a clearing license in the US. For the DCM, we've partnered with many companies: Hollywood.com, Underdog, Truth Social, MyPrize, and Fanatics. And we'll continue to look for opportunities to work together with the Solana ecosystem as well. Further, with our launch of institutional-grade custody services out of the US and Singapore, we hope to become a trusted partner to many in the Solana ecosystem looking for secure custody and execution. And finally, as we continue to build into 2026, we'll look to find ways to explore how Solana can fit into our product suite, infrastructure, and new product development. All right. Thank you.

enrichment.generated_query: Solana Strike Crypto.com Solana Breakpoint 2025 (Official Announcement Blog OR Technical Docs)

tags.impact_reason: Crypto.com's continued expansion of Solana support, including custody solutions and exchange integrations, provides more access and infrastructure for Solana projects, but isn't a groundbreaking development.

The background has immense depth, with rows of server racks fading into the darkness, creating a sense of scale and security.

editorial.headline: Crypto.com Secures First "Full Stack" CFTC Derivatives License, Integates Solana
editorial.standfirst: The exchange becomes the first major platform to hold FCM, DCM, and Clearing licenses simultaneously in the US. This regulatory moat now underpins a new wave of Solana-based institutional derivatives and white-label prediction markets.
key_takeaways: **Regulatory Moat:** Crypto.com is now the first major venue to hold US Futures Commission Merchant (FCM), Designated Contract Market (DCM), and Clearing licenses.
**Solana Derivatives:** SOL contracts are now live on CFTC-regulated "Strike" and "UpDown" options, accessible to US institutions.
**White-Label Scale:** The derivatives infrastructure is being white-labeled to partners including **Truth Social**, **Hollywood.com**, and **Fanatics**.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
While much of the industry remains in regulatory purgatory, **Crypto.com** has effectively completed its US compliance moat. In a significant disclosure at Breakpoint, Global Head of Digital Asset Partnerships **Kwon Park** confirmed the company has secured the "holy trinity" of CFTC derivatives licenses: Futures Commission Merchant (FCM), Designated Contract Market (DCM), and a Clearing license. This vertical integration allows the firm to list, broker, and clear derivatives in-house, eliminating the reliance on third-party clearinghouses that often bottlenecks institutional product development.

The immediate utility of this infrastructure is the deployment of regulated Solana derivatives in the US market. Through its "Strike" (binary options) and "UpDown" (event contracts) products, US-domiciled traders can now access regulated exposure to SOL price action. Unlike the perpetual futures found on offshore venues, these are CFTC-compliant instruments, making them investable for strict-mandate institutional capital that has previously been sidelined from the Solana volatility trade.

Perhaps the most overlooked "alpha" is the B2B2C distribution strategy. Park revealed that this derivatives engine is being white-labeled to power prediction markets for major non-crypto brands, including **Truth Social**, **Hollywood.com**, **Underdog**, and **Fanatics**. This effectively embeds crypto-railed prediction markets into mainstream consumer apps, with Solana operating as a key settlement layer within the broader ecosystem. This moves the narrative from "onboarding users to crypto apps" to "embedding crypto market structure into existing consumer giants."

Looking toward 2026, the focus shifts to institutional custody. With new custody hubs operational in Singapore and the US, Crypto.com is positioning itself not just as a retail entry point, but as a prime brokerage competitor. The combination of a CFTC-regulated execution venue and qualified custody creates a closed-loop environment for institutions to trade and settle Solana derivatives without leaving the US regulatory perimeter.
